source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Two long wires perpendicular to the \( xy \)-plane carry currents \( I \) in opposite directions. What is the magnitude of \( \vec{B} \) when \( x \gg a \)?

image_dependency: Question refers to variables x and a and opposite current directions but does not specify the wire placement in text, so the diagram likely supplies essential geometry.
image_manifest contains 1 registered image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: Solving likely requires superposing magnetic fields from two wires rather than using a single-wire formula directly.
The condition x >> a introduces an additional approximation step beyond setup from the diagram.
The provided answer has x^-2 scaling, suggesting cancellation plus far-field reasoning rather than a one-step lookup.
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with a single symbolic expression
choices has 4 options, enabling discrete answer checking
The target expression \frac{\mu_0 I a}{\pi x^2} is precise and can be algebraically compared
